steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The second team predicts that in low-visibility conditions, wider-headed fish will be less likely than narrower-headed fish to detect obstructions. The correct answer must show this head-width comparison under low-visibility conditions, with wide-headed fish performing worse.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — [no head-type comparison]: does not compare wide-headed and narrow-headed fish. ✗
  • Choice B — [bristlemouth vs. smooth-hound in low visibility]: wide-headed bristlemouth bumped into more obstructions than narrow-headed smooth-hound in medium-vortex, low-visibility conditions — directly matches the prediction. ✓
  • Choice C — [equal detection ability]: shows no difference between head types. ✗
  • Choice D — [no head-type comparison or wrong conditions]: lacks the required comparison or uses wrong conditions. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
